events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to identify the component/condition for structural bolting, where "turn-of-nut match marks absent or not rotated". The scenario: industrial site, structural bolting, work stage: rough-complete. Condition: installed-defective? The task: identify component or condition, name defect cues. So decision likely "fail" because missing match marks indicates improper tightening, which is a safety/quality defect. Risk: high or critical? Structural bolting – missing match marks could mean bolts not properly tensioned, risk of failure. Probably high or critical. Percent complete? Rough-complete means installed but not closed out, maybe 80%? But we need a number. Could be 70-80%. Value? Not specified. Confidence: 0.9? Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: "Turn-of-nut match marks absent or not rotated" – so no verification of proper tensioning. Actions: stop work, verify bolt tension with torque wrench or other method, mark per spec. Rationale: Per RCSC, match marking is required after rotation to confirm proper preload. Absence indicates bolts may be undertightened. References: RCSC Specification. component_conditions: ["installed-defective"]. So decision: fail, risk: high or critical. I'll choose critical due to structural safety. work_stage: rough-complete. percent_complete: 85% maybe? I'll put 80. value: 0. confidence: 0.95. findings: ["Turn-of-nut match marks absent or not rotated"]. actions: ["Stop bolting work", "Inspect all bolts for tension using calibrated torque wrench or direct tension indicator", "Apply match marks per RCSC specification after verifying proper tension"]. rationale: "Without match marks, there is no visual confirmation that bolts have been rotated to the required preload, posing a risk of joint failure." references: ["RCSC Specification Section 8.1 (Turn-of-Nut Method)"].

We don't need sound_source, event_time, rate, order, workable. So omit or set to empty/null. The schema has them, but the instruction says "Use ... when asked". So I'll set them to empty string, 0.0, empty list, etc. as per schema. Actually, order is an array of step-ids; I can leave empty. workable: empty array. So final JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Turn-of-nut match marks absent or not rotated"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop bolting work",
    "Inspect all bolts for tension using calibrated torque wrench or direct tension indicator",
    "Apply match marks per RCSC specification after verifying proper tension"
  ],
  "rationale": "Without match marks, there is no visual confirmation that bolts have been rotated to the required preload, posing a risk of joint failure.",
  "references": [
    "RCSC Specification Section 8.1 (Turn-of-Nut Method)"
  ]
}
